options.name: --addr
options.description: Z-80 base address of first byte after header, default=0x0000

options.name: --start
options.description: Z-80 address to disassemble from, default=addr

options.name: --end
options.description: Z-80 address to stop at, default=eof

options.name: --len
options.description: Number of bytes to disassemble (instead of --end)

options.name: --list
options.description: Generate a listing file (more detail, can't be assembled) (or, --lst)

options.name: --hex
options.description: Specify hex literal formatting (options: $, dollar, &, amper, x or h)

options.name: --html
options.description: Generates a HTML file, with hyperlinked references

options.name: --open
options.description: Automatically opens the generated file with default associated app

options.name: --markwordrefs
options.description:
Highlight with a comment literal word values (as they may be addresses)
--noCoalescStrings|ncs Don't coalesc strings

options.name: --reloffs
options.description: Show the offset of relative address mode instructions

options.name: --header
options.description: Skip N header bytes at start of file

options.name: --v
options.description:
Show version information
Numeric arguments can be in decimal (no prefix) or hex if prefixed with '0x'.
If one or more --entry arguments are specified (recommended), the file is disassembled by
following the code paths from those entry points.  All unvisited regions will be rendered
as 'DB' directives.
If the --entry argument is not specified, the file is disassembled from top to bottom.
Output is sent to stdout if no destination file specified.
Response file containing arguments can be specified using the @ prefix
